questions.question_en: When faced with a complex data set, what is your first approach?
options_en: Break it down into smaller, manageable parts
Look for patterns and trends immediately
Seek tools or software to help analyze it
Consult with others who have similar experience

questions.question_en: In a team meeting, how do you typically contribute?
options_en: I listen carefully and ask clarifying questions
I share ideas and build on others' suggestions
I take notes and summarize key points
I focus on actionable next steps

options_en: Participate in specialized conferences and webinars

options_en: Test innovative ideas on a small scale first

questions.question_en: How do you define and pursue long-term goals?
options_en: Create detailed roadmaps with intermediate milestones
Establish quantifiable metrics to measure progress

questions.question_en: How do you evaluate the implementation of new technologies?
options_en: Test on pilot projects before full adoption
Evaluate impact on existing processes and teams
Research case studies and industry best practices

mc_pillar_mapping_in_code.description: HARDCODED in XimatarAssessment.tsx lines 358-371. Maps question IDs 1-21 to pillars in SEQUENTIAL BLOCKS regardless of field.

i18n_category_labels.description: Each question in i18n has a 'category' field that cycles through pillars: q1=CompPower, q2=Comm, q3=Knowledge, q4=Creativity, q5=Drive, q6=CompPower, ... This is a ROTATING pattern, NOT sequential blocks.
i18n_category_labels.pattern: q(n) pillar = pillars[(n-1) % 5] where pillars = [comp_power, communication, knowledge, creativity, drive]

mc_score_formula_guest.formula: pillar_score = avg( (answer_index + 1) * 2.5 + random(-0.75, +0.75) ) for questions in that pillar block

mc_score_formula_authenticated.description: Raw answer_value (0-3) stored in assessment_answers table with pillar tag. Server-side computation triggered by setting completed=true on assessment_results.

ximatar_assignment.guest_mode: Top-2 pillar heuristic with hardcoded if/else chain (XimatarAssessment.tsx lines 214-236)
ximatar_assignment.authenticated_mode: Euclidean distance to 12 canonical profiles in src/lib/ximatarTaxonomy.ts (server-side)

multiple_snapshots.step1: Identify 2 weakest pillars at baseline (earliest snapshot)
multiple_snapshots.step2: delta = latest_score - baseline_score for each weak pillar

drive_computation.caching: profiles.drive_score and profiles.drive_updated_at updated via trigger trg_update_drive_on_snapshot

critical_findings.title: Pillar mapping mismatch between i18n categories and code scoring
critical_findings.description: The i18n category labels rotate through 5 pillars cyclically (q1=CompPower, q2=Comm, q3=Knowledge, q4=Creativity, q5=Drive, q6=CompPower, ...) but the hardcoded questionPillarMap in XimatarAssessment.tsx maps sequentially (q1-5=CompPower, q6-10=Comm, q11-14=Knowledge, q15-18=Creativity, q19-21=Drive). This means q2 is labeled 'Communication' in the UI but scored as 'computational_power'. This affects ALL users across ALL fields.
critical_findings.impact: Every pillar score is incorrectly computed. A user's Communication answers are scored as Computational Power, Knowledge answers scored as Communication, etc.

critical_findings.title: Guest pillar score uses same incorrect sequential block mapping
critical_findings.description: Guest scoring (lines 202-207) uses the same incorrect sequential mapping as authenticated scoring.

critical_findings.title: All 4 fields use identical pillar mapping despite different questions
critical_findings.description: The questionPillarMap is hardcoded and does not change based on assessmentSetKey. The same sequential block mapping is applied regardless of which field the user selected.

critical_findings.title: Guest XIMAtar assignment uses simple if/else heuristic, not Euclidean distance
critical_findings.description: Guest users get XIMAtar assigned via a hardcoded if/else chain based on top-2 pillars, while authenticated users use proper Euclidean distance to canonical profiles. Results may differ significantly.

critical_findings.title: Open answer has no max-length validation in UI
critical_findings.description: The Textarea for open answers has no maxLength constraint. Users could submit very long answers.
